name: Vegetable Walnut Patties
recipeIngredient: 4 slices whole wheat bread, torn into large pieces
1/2 cup basil, fresh, coarsely chopped OR 2 tsp basil, dried
2 tbsp thyme, fresh, chopped OR 1 t thyme, dried
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, bring the water to a boil over medium-high heat. Stir in the millet, cover, reduce the heat, and simmer until the water is absorbed, 20 to 30 minutes. (The millet must be freshly cooked and still warm to form the patties).
2. Meanwhile, heat the oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the celery and onion and cook, until softened, about 6 minutes. Transfer to a large bowl.
3. In a food processor fitted with the metal blade, combine the bread, walnuts, parsley, basil, thyme and tamari and process until coarsely chopped. Add to the onion/celery mixture.
4. Add the cooked millet to the onion mixture, along with the grated carrot, and stir to mix.
5. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees. Lightly oil a baking sheet.
6. Shape the warm millet mixture into 2-inch patties (about 1/2 cup) to serve as a main dish or 1-inch patties (about 1/8 cup) for an appetizer. Roll each patty in the herbed bread crumbs, patting to make the crumbs adhere, and put on the baking sheet.
7. Bake until lightly browned, 20 to 30 minutes.
8. Arrange the patties on a serving platter and garnish each one with a walnut half or a sprig of parsley.
